home.title: Read-It
home.emptyBody: Add your first book and enjoy a focused, distraction-free reading experience.
home.readAgainBody: {{title}} is finished. Restart from the first word?
home.streakKeepGoing: Keep reading every day to grow your streak.
home.tip1: Organize your reading with tags once you've uploaded your first document.
home.tip2: Reading consistently for 20 minutes a day can double your reading speed in 3 months.
home.tip3: Use the focus window to minimize distractions and improve comprehension.
home.tip4: Save vocabulary words while you read to build your personal word bank.
home.tip5: Track your streak to build a daily reading habit that sticks.
home.tip6: Reading before bed can reduce stress by up to 68% and improve sleep quality.
home.tip7: Try adjusting your reading speed to match the complexity of the material.
home.tip8: Re-reading challenging passages improves long-term retention by 40%.
home.tip9: Set a daily reading goal that feels achievable. Small wins build big habits.
home.tip10: Reading out loud engages more areas of your brain and boosts memory.
home.tip11: Take breaks every 25 minutes to keep your focus sharp and avoid fatigue.
home.tip12: Vary your reading material to build connections across different topics.
home.tip13: Highlighting key passages helps you return to important ideas later.
home.tip14: Morning readers tend to retain more information than evening readers.
home.tip15: Learning 3 new words a day adds over 1,000 words to your vocabulary each year.
home.tip16: Speed isn't everything. Comprehension and enjoyment matter most.
home.tip17: Reading regularly strengthens neural pathways and improves memory.
home.tip18: Try reading in a quiet environment to maximize focus and understanding.
home.tip19: Building a reading habit takes about 66 days on average. Keep going!
home.tip20: People who read regularly score higher on empathy and social cognition tests.
home.tip21: Pair your reading time with a ritual, like a cup of tea, to anchor the habit.
home.tip22: Reading 30 minutes daily means roughly 20 books per year.
home.tip23: Your brain processes reading differently than listening. Both are valuable.
home.tip24: Active reading means asking questions as you go. Engage with the text.
home.tip25: Preview headings and summaries before diving in for better comprehension.
home.tip26: Reading diverse authors broadens your perspective and critical thinking.
home.tip27: Even 5 minutes of reading is better than none. Every minute counts.
home.tip28: Spacing your reading sessions leads to better retention than marathon sessions.
home.tip29: Keep a reading journal to reflect on key takeaways and insights.
home.tip30: Your vocabulary grows faster through reading than any other activity.
home.tip31: Subvocalization is natural. Don't fight it — let comprehension guide your pace.
home.tip32: Reading fiction improves your ability to understand other people's emotions.
home.tip33: The more you read, the faster your brain recognizes word patterns automatically.
home.tip34: Try the Pomodoro technique: 25 minutes of focused reading, then a 5-minute break.
home.tip35: Annotating margins activates deeper processing than passive highlighting.
home.tip36: Your reading speed naturally varies with difficulty. That's a sign of good comprehension.
home.tip37: Reading in a second language strengthens both your native and target language skills.
home.tip38: Discussing what you read with others doubles your retention of key ideas.
home.tip39: Rereading a favorite book reveals details you missed the first time.
home.tip40: Consistent readers have a 23% lower risk of cognitive decline as they age.
home.tip41: Setting a specific reading time each day makes it easier to build the habit.
home.tip42: Reading long-form text trains sustained attention in a world of short content.
home.tip43: Your brain builds a mental model of what you read, like an internal simulation.
home.tip44: Mixing genres keeps your brain flexible and improves creative thinking.
home.tip45: Reading with purpose — a question in mind — dramatically improves recall.
home.tip46: Eye fixations account for 90% of reading time. Reducing backtracking speeds you up.
home.tip47: Children who see adults reading are more likely to become lifelong readers.
home.tip48: Summarizing each chapter in one sentence strengthens comprehension and memory.
home.tip49: Ambient background noise at 70 dB can boost creative reading comprehension.
home.tip50: Your working memory improves with regular reading practice over time.
home.tip51: Reading on paper may improve spatial memory of the text compared to screens.
home.tip52: Skilled readers predict upcoming words subconsciously, making them faster.
home.tip53: Taking handwritten notes while reading engages motor memory for better retention.
home.tip54: Reading before a task primes your brain with relevant vocabulary and concepts.
home.tip55: Bilingual readers often switch mental frameworks, deepening analytical thinking.
home.tip56: The spacing effect means reviewing material over days beats cramming in one session.
home.tip57: Reading aloud to someone else improves your fluency and pronunciation.
home.tip58: Curiosity-driven reading releases dopamine, making learning feel rewarding.
home.tip59: Tracking your reading progress creates a positive feedback loop that reinforces the habit.
home.tip60: Every book you finish expands your frame of reference for understanding the next one.

library.emptyNotStartedBody: All your documents have been started.

vocab.emptyNewBody: All your words have been reviewed at least once.
vocab.emptyLearningBody: Words you are actively learning will appear here.
vocab.emptyMasteredBody: Keep reviewing to move words into mastered status.
vocab.emptyBookmarkedBody: Tap the star on any word card to bookmark it.
vocab.emptyAllBody: Words you save while reading will appear here.

insight.dailyBody: Focus on Latin roots this week to expand your comprehension speed by 15%.
insight.wordReviewBody: Review this word today to reinforce your memory.

streak.onFire: Keep it going! You're on fire.
streak.archivist: You've outpaced 88% of archivists this week.

streak.impressive: An impressive streak — keep the momentum!
streak.resetBanner: No worries! Every reader has off days. Let's start fresh!

growthInsight.label: GROWTH INSIGHT
growthInsight.noSessions: Start your first reading session to unlock insights.
growthInsight.almostRecord: {{n}} more days to beat your personal record of {{record}} days!
growthInsight.speedUp: Your reading speed increased {{pct}}% in your last session. Keep it up!
growthInsight.focus: Excellent focus! You're reading with {{pct}}% concentration on average.
growthInsight.streak: You've read {{n}} days in a row. You've outpaced most readers this week!
growthInsight.consistent: Consistent reading builds vocabulary and comprehension over time. You're on the right track.

settings.title: Settings
settings.resetBody: This will restore all reading and appearance settings to their factory defaults.
settings.clearAllDataBody: This will permanently delete all documents, reading sessions, vocabulary, streaks, and preferences. You will be returned to onboarding.
settings.sectionReadingDefaultsSubtitle: These settings apply to new readings
settings.faqQ1: How do I import a PDF?
settings.faqA1: Tap the + button on the home screen, then choose "Import PDF" to select a file from your device.
settings.faqQ2: Can I adjust the reading speed?
settings.faqA2: Yes! Use the speed slider in Settings or the player controls during a reading session to set your WPM.
settings.faqQ3: How does the streak system work?
settings.faqA3: Read for any amount of time each day to maintain your streak. Missing a day resets it to zero.
settings.faqQ4: What is the daily reading goal?
settings.faqA4: It's a target number of minutes you want to read each day. You can change it from the home screen or settings.
settings.faqA5: Complex words are automatically detected during reading. You can also tap any word to save it manually.
settings.faqA6: It's a learning technique that schedules reviews at increasing intervals to help you remember words long-term.
settings.faqA7: Yes! All reading, vocabulary, and analytics work offline. Only dictionary lookups require an internet connection.
settings.faqQ8: How do I change the reading theme?
settings.faqA8: Open Settings and choose from Light, Dark, Sepia, or AMOLED reading themes under Reading Defaults.
settings.faqA9: Read-It supports PDF and TXT files. You can also paste text directly into the app.
settings.faqQ10: How do I export my data?
settings.faqA10: Go to Settings > Advanced > Export Data to save all your reading data as a JSON file.
settings.aboutTagline: Read at your pace. Grow at your speed.
settings.exportDataBody: Export all your reading data as a JSON file. This includes documents, sessions, vocabulary, and settings.
settings.clearReadingHistoryBody: This will permanently delete all reading sessions. Your library and vocabulary will be kept.
settings.clearVocabularyBody: This will permanently delete all saved vocabulary words. Everything else will be kept.
settings.clearAllDataDoubleConfirmBody: Type DELETE to confirm. This action cannot be undone.

onboarding.subtitle: Your digital sanctuary for deep reading and knowledge retention. Escape the noise and rediscover the rhythm of words.
onboarding.assessmentSubtitle: Select a level to set your initial pace.
onboarding.importSubtitle: Transform any document into a beautiful, focused reading experience.
onboarding.levelBeginnerDesc: Focusing on comprehension and steady word absorption. Perfect for deep study.
onboarding.levelIntermediateDesc: The standard conversational pace. Ideal for fiction and general non-fiction enjoyment.
onboarding.levelAdvancedDesc: A swift, active reading style. For those who process information rapidly and effectively.
onboarding.levelExpertDesc: High-speed scanning and retention. For the seasoned bibliophile and speed-reader.
